Angela Erdmann never met her grandfather. He died in 1946, six years before she was born. But on Tuesday, April 8th, 2014, Angela told the story of an amazing moment. She received a message in a bottle, 101 years after her grandfather threw it into the Baltic Sea.

This message in a bottle is believed to be the oldest in the world. A museum in Germany is now showing it, and they gave the bottle to Angela.

“It was very surprising,” said Angela Erdmann, who is 62 years old. She remembered how she learned about the bottle. “A man came to my door and told me he had mail from my grandfather. He explained that someone found a message in a bottle and my grandfather’s name was on the card.”

The man who visited her was a family history researcher. He found Angela in Berlin after the museum in Hamburg, a northern port city, got the letter.

A fisherman named Konrad Fischer found the brown beer bottle in the Baltic Sea near Kiel, a city in the north. The bottle had been in the water for 101 years.

Holger von Neuhoff, who works at the museum, said this is the oldest message in a bottle he has seen. “We have older documents that were found without bottles, but with both a bottle and a document, this is the oldest so far. It is in very good condition,” he said.

For almost 100 years, police and intelligence agencies have used polygraphs, also called lie detectors, to help catch criminals, spies, and traitors. Polygraphs are often shown in movies, where guilty people look very nervous while being tested.

However, polygraphs might not be used for much longer. Scientists in Britain and the Netherlands have created a new way to find out if someone is lying. Their method works over 70% of the time in tests and could be used in police stations around the world within ten years.

Instead of looking at a person’s face or how they speak or move their arms, the new method watches how the whole body moves. This can show signs that someone feels guilty. In the United States, polygraphs are often used in criminal cases and for jobs in places like the FBI and CIA, but they are not as common in Europe. Many scientists and lawyers don’t trust that polygraphs always work well.

The new method has done well in experiments. The main idea is that people who lie move around, or fidget, more than people who tell the truth. The researchers use a special suit, similar to ones used in movies to track body movement, to measure this fidgeting. One of the researchers, Ross Anderson, explained that, in the past, people trying to tell if someone is lying were right only a little more than half the time. But with the new method, it works over 70% of the time, and sometimes even over 80%.

Anderson said, "The important thing to remember is that guilty people fidget more, and we can measure this well."

The polygraph was invented in 1921 by a policeman named John Larson, who used ideas from psychologist William Marston. The polygraph measures changes in how fast your heart beats, your blood pressure, how much you sweat, and how you breathe to try to tell if you are lying. Even though movies often show polygraphs as very accurate, the US Supreme Court said in 1998 that people do not agree on if they really work. The US National Academy of Scientists also agreed in 2003 that polygraphs are not always reliable.

Do you want your child to be good at sports, join the school team, or maybe even play for their country one day? If yes, it may help if your child is born in November—or, if not, in October. A study has shown that children born in these months are fitter than other children in their class.

Children with birthdays in November and October were found to be fitter, stronger, and more powerful than those born in other months, especially compared to children born in April or June.

Dr. Gavin Sandercock, a scientist from Essex University, led the study. He found that children born in the autumn (October and November) have a clear physical advantage over other children.

The study included 8,550 boys and girls, between 10 and 16 years old, from 26 schools in Essex. Testing took place from 2007 to 2010. The children were tested for three things: stamina, handgrip strength, and lower-body power.

The results showed that the month a child is born can make a big difference in their fitness, muscle strength, and how quickly they can move. These things are important for being good at sports.

November-born children did the best—they had the most stamina and power, and were the second strongest. Children born in October were also very fit. They were the strongest and the third best in power. December-born children also did fairly well.

In some cases, there was a big difference between children born in different months but who were in the same class. For example, a boy born in November can run 10% faster, jump 12% higher, and is 15% more powerful than a boy born in April. This is a big advantage.

These differences may decide who gets chosen for top sports teams, because small differences in fitness often matter a lot at high levels.

The study, published in the International Journal of Sports Medicine, also found that children born in April were the least fit, with children born in June also not doing well. This could mean that these children are less likely to be picked for school teams and may not do as well in sports.

The small room looks like a classroom. Its walls are made of corrugated metal. There are charts on the walls showing the alphabet and a map of Bangladesh. But there is always loud hammering noise, and the air is full of strong chemicals that hurt the eyes and make it hard to breathe. This makes it difficult to pay attention.

The children in this tiny classroom are lucky. They do not have to work in the factories nearby. For 14 years, SOHAY, a local group helped by the Global Fund for Children and Comic Relief, has been working in poor parts of Dhaka. SOHAY tries to help children who work in dangerous places, like aluminum and plastic factories or tanneries, to go to school instead.

This classroom is one of 23 centers in the city that SOHAY has started. The centers help children catch up on learning so they can go to primary school. After they start school, they can come to the center to do their homework and get help from other students.

Alamin, who is ten years old, used to work in a plastic factory. Now he goes to one of the centers. His father sells things on the street, and his mother works as a cleaner part-time. They are all happy that Alamin goes to school and does not do dangerous work anymore. His friend Rabi, who also went to the factory, says, “I like school. I want to forget working in the factory.”

SOHAY also talks to parents and business owners to stop child labor. They teach new skills to help families earn more money. “It is very hard to get working children into school,” says Mohammed al-Mamun, who manages SOHAY’s programs. “They are not the same as other children. Sometimes they find it hard to make friends and get used to school after leaving work.”

It is also very hard to make sure they keep going to school. Many of these children stop going before finishing.

Zhorna Akter Sumayya is seven years old. She has two older brothers. One works in a restaurant and the other works in a club. But Zhorna went to a SOHAY center and now goes to a public primary school. Her family needs the money her brothers earn, but her parents wanted their daughter to go to school.

A big argument has started around the world about whether the native people of Bolivia can chew coca leaves. Coca leaves are used to make cocaine. This argument may affect drug laws everywhere. 

Bolivia has received special permission from the 1961 Single Convention on Narcotic Drugs, a global agreement about drug control, to let its native people chew coca leaves. Bolivia said that the old agreement goes against its new law, made in 2009, which says the country must protect coca leaves as part of its culture and history. Bolivia also says that coca leaves in their natural form are not a dangerous drug.

People in South America have chewed coca leaves for hundreds of years. They say the leaves give them extra energy and help with health problems. People who agree with Bolivia believe the country is doing the right thing by standing up for native people’s rights. One supporter, Danny Kushlick from the Transform Drug Policy Foundation, called Bolivia’s decision “inspirational and groundbreaking.” He said it shows that countries can change how they deal with international drug agreements if they want to stop the war on drugs.

But the International Narcotics Control Board (INCB), which makes sure countries follow the world’s drug laws, said Bolivia’s actions could be a problem for international drug control. Several countries, including the UK, US, Italy, Sweden, the Netherlands, and Russia, are against Bolivia’s demands. The UK told the United Nations that it understands and respects that coca leaves are important in Bolivian culture. But the UK is worried that this special rule could make coca farming increase and also make it easier for coca to be used for making cocaine. They think this could hurt the global fight against illegal drugs and make it harder for countries around the world to work together to stop drug crime.

On Black Fell, a hill looking over the beautiful Kielder Water in Northumberland, a group of people wait in a car park beside a strange wooden building. The building looks very modern and simple. This is Kielder Observatory, a special place for people who want to look at the stars. The people waiting outside are lucky, because many more people wanted to visit, but only a few can come at once.

Inside the observatory, where it is warm and softly lit, the man who started the observatory and is also the main astronomer, Gary Fildes, talks to his team. Gary used to be a bricklayer and has long hair. He encourages his team and together they talk about maybe seeing the northern lights, but Gary is not sure if they will. Instead, they decide to look at the planets Jupiter and Venus with their big telescopes, and later find stars like Capella and Betelgeuse. Another special thing they hope to see is the International Space Station moving across the sky. Gary reminds his team that their job is not just to show the stars, but also to make it fun and exciting for visitors.

Gary, who is 49, is a leader in the UK's growing astrotourism industry, which means bringing people to visit places where the night sky is very dark and full of stars. Northumberland became very important for astrotourism in 2013. That year, the national park with Hadrian’s Wall, Kielder Water, and Kielder Forest Park, which covers about 1,500 square kilometers, was named a Dark Sky Park. This is the only one in England.

Dark Sky Parks are very rare. In 2013, a study found that only 5% of people in the UK could see more than 31 stars at a time because of city lights. To become a Dark Sky Park, a place must work hard to stop light pollution, which means too much man-made light at night. They must also show that their sky is really dark at night. In Northumberland, the sky is so dark that even the planet Venus can make a shadow on the ground.

Duncan Wise, who helps visitors at Northumberland National Park, worked hard to win this special title. He said that most people only look at the land up to the horizon, but the sky above is important too. Duncan and others spent years collecting information and joining with local councils, parks, and community groups. Together, they made a plan to control outside lights, especially for new buildings

When Larry Pizzi first heard about electric bikes almost 20 years ago, he wondered, “Why would anyone want to ruin a bike by adding a motor and batteries?” Many people who like regular bikes still do not understand e-bikes. Bike shops have not sold many e-bikes, even though they have been around since the late 1990s. Not many have been sold in the US. But Pizzi, who is now the boss of Currie Technologies, the top e-bike seller in the US, thinks this is about to change. Others in the bike industry agree. Well-known brands like Trek, Raleigh, and Specialized all sell electric bikes now, and they think people will buy more of them soon. “We’re about to see a lot more people using e-bikes,” Pizzi said. “More companies are starting to sell them each month.”

The US is different from other countries with e-bikes. Almost 32 million e-bikes were sold in 2014, mostly in China, where people use them to get around. E-bikes are also popular in many European countries. They are commonly used in the Netherlands and Switzerland. German postal workers use them for their jobs, and BMW sells one for about $3,000.

Electric bikes are not the same as motorcycles or mopeds, which run only on motors. Electric bikes are regular bikes that you can pedal, but you can also use the motor to help you. Riding an e-bike feels like riding a normal bike with a strong wind behind you. The motor helps you go faster or climb hills, but you still pedal. E-bikes are usually allowed on bike paths, and they cannot go faster than 20 miles per hour.

This is the life of a modern chemist: you wait a long time for a new element to be discovered, and then four are found at once. Scientists in Japan, Russia, and the US discovered these four new elements. They are the first new elements added to the periodic table since 2011, when elements 114 and 116 were added. These new elements do not last long and are very radioactive. They also finish the seventh row of the periodic table, which means science textbooks everywhere are now out of date.

The International Union of Pure and Applied Chemistry (IUPAC), a group in the US that decides how chemicals should be named and measured, officially recognized the new elements on December 30, 2015. They did this after carefully looking at research done since 2004.

The scientists who discovered the elements now need to choose official names for them. Right now, the elements are called ununtrium, ununpentium, ununseptium, and ununoctium. These are not real names; they just show the atomic numbers 113, 115, 117, and 118. An element’s atomic number is the number of protons in its nucleus.

IUPAC said that scientists from Russia and the US, working together, gave enough proof for the discovery of elements 115, 117, and 118. For element 113, which both Russia and America also claimed, the credit went to a team from RIKEN Institute in Japan. This means Japan is the first Asian country allowed to name an element.

According to IUPAC rules, new elements can be named after ideas from myths, minerals, places or countries, or scientists.

Showing a car that can only go 25mph, has two seats, and has no pedals or steering wheel may not seem exciting at a car show. However, Google in the United States surprised the car and taxi industries when it showed its newest self-driving car. 

This small electric car, which Google is testing near its office in Mountain View, California, does not have normal controls like foot pedals. Instead, you use a smartphone app to tell the car where to go, and there is a big STOP button between the two seats if you need to stop the car quickly.

The car drives itself. It does everything: finds the way, turns the wheels, speeds up, and slows down. Google is making about 100 of these cars to test over the next two years.

Sergey Brin, Google’s co-founder, said that the car is “still a test model” but the goal is “to help people who have trouble getting around today.” He said that riding in the car was “very relaxing,” and after a few seconds inside, he forgot he was even in a car. He compared it to riding a chairlift alone, which he finds peaceful.

Google says the main goal of this project is to make cars safer. The car is designed with soft foam on the front and a plastic front window to protect people if the car hits someone.

A company in Detroit (name not given yet) has specially built these cars. Google will use them to learn more about how to make driverless cars work better. For now, these cars are only for testing and are not for sale.

Galina Zaglumyonova was asleep in her apartment in the middle of Chelyabinsk when a huge explosion woke her up. The blast broke her balcony windows and smashed the pots holding her few houseplants. When she got out of bed, she saw a big white streak in the morning sky and heard car alarms going off on the street below. "I didn’t understand what was happening," she said. “There was a loud bang and then lots of smaller booms. I thought maybe a plane had crashed.”

But what really happened was that a big meteorite, weighing ten tons, fell to Earth in burning pieces just after sunrise. Officials said almost 1,200 people were hurt, and more than 40 went to the hospital. Most people were injured by broken glass from the shock wave made by the falling meteorite. No one died.

The meteorite entered the Earth’s atmosphere at a speed of at least 33,000 miles per hour and broke into pieces between 18 and 32 miles above the ground, according to the Russian Academy of Sciences. This event scared many people in Chelyabinsk, a city in Russia with one million people, south of the Ural mountains. Many tried to call their families and friends, and the phone networks quickly stopped working.

Many people filmed the event, and the videos often included scared shouting and bad language. The pieces of the meteorite glowed brighter as they got closer to the ground. The white streak it left in the sky could be seen from hundreds of miles away, even in the nearby country of Kazakhstan.

Tatyana Bets was working at the front desk of a hospital in the city center when the meteorite hit. “We first noticed the wind, then the room was filled with a very bright light, and we saw a strange smoke cloud in the sky,” she said. A few minutes later, there were explosions.

The Ministry of the Interior said at least three holes were found in the ground afterwards. The army was watching these places. One hole was more than six meters wide. Another piece of the meteorite crashed through the thick ice of a nearby lake. Local army officials said there was no danger from radiation at the places where the meteorite hit.

An octopus escaped from the National Aquarium in New Zealand. It got out of its tank, went down a 50-meter drainpipe, and disappeared into the sea.

This scene was like something from the movie Finding Nemo. The octopus, named Inky, escaped because someone accidentally left the lid of his tank a little bit open. At night, when no one was there, Inky climbed out of his glass tank, went down the side, and moved across the floor.

Rob Yarrell, the manager of the aquarium, said, “Octopuses are famous for escaping. I don’t think Inky was sad or lonely, because octopuses like being alone. He is just very curious and wanted to know what was outside his tank. That’s just what he is like.”

People at the aquarium think Inky moved across the floor for about three or four meters. Then he found a drainpipe that led straight to the sea. The pipe was 50 meters long and opened to the waters of Hawke’s Bay, on New Zealand’s North Island.

Another idea is that Inky squeezed into an open pipe at the top of his tank, which led to the drain. 

“When we came in the next morning and saw the empty tank, I was really surprised,” said Yarrell. He and his staff felt sad, but said Inky always acted in surprising ways.

Reiss Jenkinson, who works at the aquarium, is sure that no one stole Inky. He said, “I know how octopuses act. I’ve seen them escape from tight places before. Also, our security is very good, and there is no reason someone would take Inky.”

Because octopuses have no bones, they can squeeze through very small spaces, even gaps as small as a coin. Octopuses are also very smart and can use tools.

At another place called the Island Bay Marine Education Center in Wellington, an octopus was seen visiting another tank at night to steal crabs, then going back to its own tank. Another octopus there, named Ozymandias, opened a jar faster than any other octopus before being released into the sea.

Insects are crunchy, have lots of protein, and can be found under rocks nearby. People in most places have not eaten insects as food, but this is starting to change. Soon, insects may be more common in our meals.

In spring 2013, there will be efforts to help people get used to the idea of eating insects. The goal is to put insects in grocery stores, not just in fancy restaurants. In April, there will be a festival in London called Pestival 2013. This event is to help people learn to appreciate insects, and eating them will be a big part of the festival. The Nordic Food Lab, the group behind the famous Danish restaurant Noma, will run a special two-day restaurant at the festival. In 2012, this team served ants at Claridge’s hotel in London, and the event was very popular.

In March, a BBC documentary will show food writer Stefan Gates finding and eating insects like deep-fried locusts and barbecued tarantulas.

Even though some people might joke about eating bugs, there is a serious reason behind this. Many experts say eating insects could help the environment. Since 2011, the United Nations’ Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) has paid for projects that support eating and farming insects in southeast Asia and Africa. In these places, about two billion people already eat insects and caterpillar larvae often. In 2012, the FAO made a list of 1,909 kinds of edible insects. With money from the Dutch government, the FAO is planning a big meeting in 2013 to talk about insects as an important food.

In the next 30 years, there will be nine billion people on Earth. Right now, one billion people do not have enough food. As the number of people grows, we will need more land, water, forests, fish, and other resources. Meat is becoming more expensive, not only because of money, but also because rainforests are being destroyed to create space for cattle to graze or to grow food for them. Cows also make methane gas, which is bad for the environment. Raising animals for meat causes a lot of greenhouse gas pollution—35% of the planet’s methane, 65% of its nitrous oxide, and 9% of its carbon dioxide.

The official eight-hour workday is finished. The most important tasks have been completed; anything left can wait until tomorrow. At this point, many workers would think about going home. However, for millions of workers in Japan, leaving work on time and being home for dinner might make them seem disloyal to their company.

For many years, Japanese companies have expected workers to work very hard, often for long hours. Now, the government wants to change this culture of overwork. They are thinking about making a new law that would require workers to take at least five days of paid holiday each year.

Currently, Japanese workers can take about 18.5 days of paid holiday each year, close to the world average. The minimum is ten paid days plus 15 public holidays. In reality, most workers only use about nine of those paid days.

While workers in the UK see a two-week summer holiday as normal and their right, many Japanese workers think that even taking a four-day holiday is too much. This new law, if approved, is meant to help Japanese workers take more holiday, like British workers who use about 20 paid days a year, or French workers who take about 25.

Japan’s tough work culture has helped make the country successful in business, and Japanese workers are respected for their loyalty. But because of this focus on work, people often do not have time for other parts of life. Japan's low birth rate and shrinking population happen partly because couples do not have enough time together to start families. More workers are getting sick from stress, or even dying from too much work, a problem known as karoshi.

Even though research shows that longer hours do not always lead to better work, many Japanese workers still feel the pressure to work like people did in the busy 1980s.

An old man living alone in a crater on the moon is the main character in John Lewis’s Christmas advertisement for 2015. This is surprising, and the department store is using this story to help a charity in its new expensive campaign.

Every year, John Lewis’s Christmas ad is a big event. For many people, it means the start of Christmas shopping. This year, John Lewis wants to use this attention to raise a lot of money for the charity Age UK. The company also hopes that staff and customers will join their local Age UK groups to help older people who might be alone during the holidays.

John Lewis has spent £7 million on this campaign. It includes a professional TV ad, a smartphone game, and special products like glow-in-the-dark pyjamas. In 11 stores, there are areas decorated to look like the moon.

For the last two years, John Lewis’s Christmas ads have shown soft animals, like a bear and hare, and then a penguin. This year, the ad tells a touching story about a young girl named Lily. She uses her telescope to see an old man living alone on the moon. Lily tries to send him a letter and even a note with a bow and arrow. Finally, she sends him a present — a telescope attached to balloons — which helps them connect.

The message of the ad is: “Show someone they’re loved this Christmas,” which is similar to Age UK’s campaign: “No one should have no one at Christmas.” Money made from selling a mug, a gift tag, and a card will go to Age UK.

Rachel Swift, the head of marketing at John Lewis, said that this charity means a lot to people at this time of year. She hopes the ad makes people think about someone who lives nearby and might not see anyone else.

The music in the ad is a version of the song "Half the World Away" by the band Oasis, sung by a little-known Norwegian singer named Aurora. It cost £1 million to make the advert. The moon scenes were filmed at Warner Bros Studios, where the Harry Potter movies were made. The set was designed by someone who worked on the new Star Wars film, The Force Awakens.

Before launching the ad, John Lewis got people excited by showing short previews on TV and social media with the hashtag #OnTheMoon. There will also be a full moon on Christmas Day in 2015, but this was just a coincidence, said Swift.

A nasal spray containing the “love hormone” oxytocin might help children with autism handle social situations better, according to US researchers. Brain scans of children with autism showed that just one dose of oxytocin helped their brains respond better to people’s facial expressions. This could make social situations feel easier and more enjoyable for them.

The scientists said that using oxytocin could make behavioral therapies for autism work better. These therapies already help people with autism learn how to handle social situations. “Over time, children might respond better in social situations, show more interest in being with others, make more eye contact, and have better conversations,” said Kevin Pelphrey, head of the Child Neuroscience Lab at Yale University.

Autism is a condition seen in more than one in 100 people. People with autism often have trouble talking with others and understanding social situations. There is no cure yet for these social problems.

The Yale researchers studied oxytocin as a possible treatment because this brain chemical helps people bond and trust each other. Results from different studies are mixed. For example, one study showed that several days of oxytocin did not help young people much. But Pelphrey said oxytocin might help the brain learn from social experiences, especially when used with therapy to encourage social behavior.

“Our study shows that oxytocin affects the brain and, together with therapy, could make social skills improve,” Pelphrey said.

The scientists scanned the brains of 17 young people with autism, aged 8 to 16, while they looked at pictures of cars or photos showing people’s eyes and emotions. The scans were done 45 minutes after the children took either a spray of oxytocin or a fake (placebo) spray.

The scans showed that after taking oxytocin, the reward centers in the brain worked more normally. These parts of the brain were more active when the children looked at faces and less active when they looked at cars.

This study was published in the journal Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences from the United States.

Benjamin Carle is made 96.9% in France, right down to his underwear and socks. However, six Ikea forks, a guitar from China, and paint with no clear origin stopped him from being 100% French-made, but nobody is perfect. 

In 2013, Carle, who was 26 years old, tried to live using only products made in France for ten months. This was for a TV documentary. He got the idea after the Minister for Economic Renewal, Arnaud Montebourg, asked people to buy French products to help save French factories.

Doing this experiment was hard for Carle. He had to give up his smartphone, TV, and fridge (all made in China), his glasses (made in Italy), his underpants (from Morocco), his morning coffee (from Guatemala), and his favorite David Bowie music (from Britain). Luckily, his girlfriend Anaïs and his cat Loon (both French) stayed with him.

Carle said, “Politicians say things and expect us to agree. I wanted to see if what the minister asked was really possible.” He said this to The Guardian, while drinking non-French coffee in Paris, after finishing his documentary.

Carle made three rules for himself: only eat food made in France, not use anything made outside France, and live on €1,800 a month (which is more than the minimum wage of €1,430, because it is expensive to live in Paris).

When Carle started, he was surprised to find out that only 4.5% of the things in his apartment were made in France. He had to get rid of everything else, like his lightbulbs (from China) and green beans (from Kenya). After moving out the foreign things, his home was almost empty.

Because no fridges are made in France, Carle had to keep his food cold on the window ledge. Without French nail clippers, he used a penknife for his toenails. He replaced his clothes, even his underwear, with more expensive French-made options: €26 for underpants, €9 for socks, €75 for a polo shirt, €26 for espadrille sandals, but he could not buy jeans because none are made in France.

During the ten months, Carle searched stores for 100% French products, learned to cook French seasonal fruits and vegetables, was happy to brush his teeth with a French-made toothbrush (made in Picardie by a company with 29 workers), and washed his underwear by

The Moroccan city of Ouarzazate is used to hosting big movie productions. It is near the Sahara Desert and is the center of Morocco’s film industry, known as “Ouallywood.” Now, this trading city is becoming famous for something else: a group of four big solar power plants. Together with water and wind power, these plants will help Morocco get almost half of its electricity from renewable energy by 2020. If there is extra electricity, Morocco hopes to sell some to Europe.

This project is an important part of Morocco’s plan to use its deserts to become a world leader in solar energy. When all four parts are finished, it will be the largest concentrated solar power plant in the world. The first part, called Noor 1, will start working in November 2015.

The plant uses large mirrors to collect sunlight. This method is less common and more expensive than the solar panels you often see on building roofs, but it has one big advantage: it can keep making electricity even after sunset.

As engineers finish working on Noor 1, its 500,000 crescent-shaped mirrors shine in the desert sun. There are 800 rows of mirrors, and they move quietly every few minutes to follow the sun as it moves across the sky. When all four sections are done, the solar plants at Ouarzazate will cover as much land as Morocco’s capital city, Rabat, and will produce 580 megawatts of electricity—enough for one million homes.

People have known for many years that the desert can produce a lot of solar power. After the Chernobyl nuclear accident in 1986, a German scientist named Gerhard Knies found out that the world’s deserts get more energy from the sun in a few hours than people use in an entire year. The difficult part is collecting this energy and bringing it to the cities and towns where people need it.

Experts say that by 2020, solar energy will make up one third of Morocco’s renewable energy, and wind and water power will each make up another third. Morocco’s environment minister, Hakima el-Haite, said: “We are very proud of this project. I think it is the most important solar plant in the world.”

There are now more tigers in Nepal than at any time since the 1970s, according to a new count. This is good news for people trying to save tigers, because in south Asia, tiger numbers have been falling for 100 years. The number of wild royal Bengal tigers in Nepal has now reached 198. This is a 63.6% increase in just five years, said the government report.

Maheshwar Dhakal, a scientist working to protect Nepal’s wildlife, said, “This is very good news.” The number of tigers was counted by looking at pictures from over 500 cameras. These cameras were placed in five special protected areas and three places where animals move from one area to another. More than 250 wildlife experts worked on this report, which cost about £250,000.

In Asia, more people now have money to spend, so the price of tiger skins and body parts used in traditional Chinese medicine is higher. International groups pay poor people in Nepal a lot of money to kill tigers. The tiger skin and bones are passed to people who take them across the open border to India, where most of the big buyers are. One big problem is that some powerful people help these groups and are involved in this illegal trade.

Experts say the bigger number of tigers is because Nepal is protecting the parks better, fighting poaching, and taking care of the forests where tigers live. About 29% of Nepal is covered with forest. But experts say Nepal still needs to do more to protect the places where tigers live and the animals that tigers hunt, so that tigers have enough space and food.

As the number of tigers grows, there have also been more problems between tigers and villagers. In 2012, tigers killed seven people near national parks, compared to four people in 2011, park workers said. Now, villagers also want better safety.

Robert Myslajek stops suddenly. He finds what he is looking for between two paw prints on a muddy mountain path. “Droppings!” he says excitedly. Seeing wolves is so rare that even finding their poop is a good day for a trained animal tracker. But now it is getting easier to find signs of wolves. There are now about 1,500 wolves in Poland. This number has doubled in the last 15 years. Along with the brown bear, lynx, and wolverine, wolves are one of the last big meat-eating animals in Europe.

Animal experts from the UK, Germany, and the Netherlands are coming to Poland to learn how the country has protected and saved its wolves, which are often seen as bad animals, even in fairy tales. Myslajek, a scientist who often appears on Polish television, says wolves are amazing. They can travel up to 30 kilometers in one hunt. “The Beskidy pack is a strong family group, with eight or nine wolves. This year, we found five babies, two year-old wolves, and two adults. We watch them with cameras in the forest and by looking for their tracks in the mud and snow. In each pack, only one pair of wolves has babies each year, and all the other members help look after the young with great care and teamwork.”

Myslajek, whose father was a shepherd, does not understand why wolves are thought of as dangerous or evil. “Why do we call someone a 'lone-wolf gunman'? Why did stories like Little Red Riding Hood have a big bad wolf?” He is very interested in these wild animals, who have stayed in the wilderness for 33,000 years, while other wolves became pet dogs. Wolves are not pets. Myslajek says that only science can save Europe’s wild meat-eating animals, especially wolves that many people do not like. Wolves help forests and keep the numbers of other wild animals under control. “Natural predators keep the balance in nature. They stop plant-eaters from eating too many trees or plants, so trees can grow tall for birds.”

Hunting wolves was stopped in the western Carpathian mountains in 1995 and in all of Poland by 1998. Now, there are wolf packs in almost every large forest in the country. These wild animals live freely with humans, not behind fences like in African wildlife parks.

Illegal downloading is like stealing, just as much as taking someone’s wallet is stealing, says author Philip Pullman. In an article for Index on Censorship, Pullman, who leads the Society of Authors, strongly supports copyright laws. He criticizes people who download music or books for free from the Internet.

He says the technology is amazing, but people forget that downloading without paying is wrong. He calls it stealing, just like taking a wallet from someone’s pocket. Pullman says it is very unfair that people can take an artist’s work without paying and not get punished.

His article was published after music industry leaders talked with British Prime Minister David Cameron about online piracy. Pullman, who wrote the His Dark Materials books, says that authors and musicians work hard for many years, often earning little money, to create things people enjoy. But once their work becomes popular, they lose the chance to make a living because of illegal downloading.

He finishes by saying, “The rule is simple and doesn’t change with new technology: if we want to enjoy someone’s work, we should pay for it.”

Pullman is writing in a magazine published by a campaign group, where he talks with Cathy Casserly, leader of Creative Commons, an organization that helps creators manage their copyright. Casserly believes that copyright laws have problems because they were made a long time ago, before the Internet.

She says that copyright rules stop people from easily sharing or changing other people’s work, even though there are many new ways to do this now. Casserly thinks artists should find new ways to share and earn money from their work. She mentions science fiction writer Cory Doctorow, who said, “My problem is not piracy, it’s obscurity,” meaning he worries more about people not knowing his work than about people copying it.

People from all over Rwanda, and even from nearby Burundi, come to the southern town of Butare to visit a small shop called Inzozi Nziza, which means “Sweet Dreams.” They come to try something new that most of them have never had before – ice cream, which is cold, sweet, and smooth.

In this shop, which is the first ice-cream parlor in the central African country, people can buy ice cream in different flavors like sweet cream, passion fruit, strawberry, and pineapple. They can also add toppings like fresh fruit, honey, chocolate chips, and granola. The shop also sells black tea and coffee. The sign outside the shop says, “ice cream, coffee, dreams.” Inzozi Nziza is making many people curious about ice cream and, at the same time, is “changing lives,” says Louise Ingabire, who manages the shop.

Louise says, “Ice cream is important. Some Rwandans like it, but it’s still new here. We need to let more people know that they will like it too.” The shop’s motto is to make dreams come true, and for Louise, this has happened. “Before, I did not have a job. I stayed at home. Now, I can see good things for my future. I earn money and can help my family,” says Louise, who is 27 years old.

Butare has about 89,600 people and is 135 kilometers south of Rwanda’s capital, Kigali. It is also where the National University of Rwanda is located. Inzozi Nziza has become a favorite place for tired students who want to relax and enjoy something nice and cool. A 24-year-old student named Kalisa Migendo says, “This place brings people together. If you want to talk with a friend, or with a girl or boy, you can come to Inzozi Nziza for ice cream.”

Most of the ingredients for the ice cream are bought locally, and the milk comes from a nearby town called Nyanza. The shop brings in vanilla beans and cocoa from other countries.

People call him the Robin Hood of the banks. This man took out many loans from banks, worth almost half a million euros, but never planned to pay the money back. Instead, Enric Duran gave the money to projects that supported ideas different from capitalism.

After hiding for 14 months, Duran is not sorry for what he did, even though he might go to jail because of it. “I’m proud of what I did,” he said in an interview on Skype from a secret place.

From 2006 to 2008, Duran took out 68 loans for business and personal reasons from 39 banks in Spain. He gave the money to social activists, paid for talks against capitalism, and bought TV cameras for a media group.

“I saw that these social movements were trying to build new ideas, but they didn’t have enough money or ways to communicate,” he said. “At the same time, the economy kept growing, creating a system where money was made from nothing.”

Duran said that taking these loans from banks was his way of showing what was wrong with the system. At first, he started carefully. “I filled out a few credit forms with my real information. They said no, but I wanted to learn what they needed,” he said.

Soon, he built a complicated plan using many accounts, payments, and transfers. “I was always learning new things.” By the summer of 2007, he understood how the system worked. He started applying for loans using a fake television company. “Then, I got a lot,” he said.

In total, he got €492,000.

Scientists studied DNA from a wisdom tooth of a European hunter-gatherer, giving them a rare look at how modern humans were before farming began. This man lived in Spain about 7,000 years ago. His DNA showed he had blue eyes, dark skin, and black or brown hair. He probably could not digest milk (lactose intolerant) and had more trouble eating starchy foods than later farmers, who changed what people ate when they started farming.

Farming brought people and animals closer together, which helped humans get better immune systems to fight new diseases passed from animals. But scientists thought farming had a bigger effect on human immune systems than it really did. When they tested the hunter-gatherer’s DNA, they found he already had some genes that protected him from diseases, and some of these genes are still in Europeans today.

Carles Lalueza-Fox, who led the study, said he was surprised by what they found. The study began after cavers found two skeletons in a deep cave in the Cantabrian Mountains in northwest Spain in 2006. The bodies, from two men about 30 years old, were well preserved by the cold cave. Tests showed the remains were about 7,000 years old, before farming came to Europe from the Middle East. This matched old objects found in the cave, such as reindeer teeth with holes in them, which people used to decorate their clothes.

Agios Efstratios is a small, far-away island. It is forgotten by banks, the government, and most people today. There is no mobile phone internet, no bank machines (ATMs), and no credit card machines on the island.

Before Greece’s financial problems, people on Agios Efstratios lived quite well. They used the post office for banking. Every summer, all the small rooms for rent were full of people who had heard about the island’s empty beaches, clean sea, and fresh seafood. Most visitors came after hearing about the island from friends.

But now, because banks in Greece have closed, life on Agios Efstratios has become very hard. People must travel nine hours to the nearest big island to get cash. Greek tourists also cannot get enough cash to visit. “Tourist numbers are down 80% this year,” said Mayor Maria Kakali. She works in a small office in her village, where about 200 people live. “Even people who are from here and now live in Athens are not coming, even though they have their own homes on the island.”

Mayor Kakali has asked the government and a big bank to bring an ATM to the island soon. But she is worried it will come too late for this year’s tourist season. Tourism is the most important way people make money here. Fishing and farming are much smaller.

“We have almost no bookings for August,” said Kakali. “Usually, we are full and people call asking for rooms, but we can’t help them.”

A difficult winter may be a bit easier because 50 workers will be staying to work on the harbor. But there is an even bigger problem coming. The government plans to end a tax break that the island has had for many years.

About 50,000 mystery shopping visits happen every month in the UK, according to the Mystery Shopping Providers Association. Because more people are shopping online, more stores now want mystery shoppers.

Mystery shoppers pretend to be regular customers in a shop, but they are really there to check how good or bad the customer service is. Simon Boydell from Marketforce says stores know that people who go to a real shop want something special they can't get online. The shops want to know how well their staff give this experience to shoppers.

Jill Spencer from ABa, a mystery shopping company, says they send shoppers to different stores and make sure they do not go back to the same shop for at least three months. Each day, a mystery shopper usually goes to five to ten stores for up to eight hours, and then spends one or two more hours writing detailed reports about their visits.

Mystery shoppers can earn up to £155 a day. They also get money back for fuel, hotel stays, and wear and tear on their car. Some shoppers drive as much as 20,000 miles a year. Video mystery shoppers, who use hidden cameras in a button or a bag, can earn even more—up to £300 a day.

Usually, shoppers get back any money they spend in the shops. Laura, a 50-year-old mystery shopper from Devon, says she is usually given between £5 and £20 to spend at each store. She needs to use this money to test the service at the counter. The thing she buys is often linked to what the store wants her to check. Laura is told a story, like buying something from a special area of the shop or from a new product range, but most of the time she can choose what to buy—and she gets to keep it.

Vienna is the best city in the world to live in, while Baghdad is the worst. London, Paris, and New York are not even in the top 35, according to a worldwide study about quality of life. Cities where people speak German are at the top of the 18th Mercer Quality of Life study, with Vienna, Zurich, Munich, Dusseldorf, and Frankfurt all in the best seven cities.

Paris dropped down the list. It is now number 37, ten places lower than before. London is just behind at number 39. This is mostly because both cities are seen as more at risk for terrorist attacks.

The study looked at things like social and economic conditions, health, education, housing, and the environment. Big companies use this study to decide where to put their offices or factories and how much to pay their workers.

Helena Hartlauer, who was born in Vienna, said she was not surprised that her city is number one. Vienna’s city government has spent a lot of money on good and affordable social housing for a long time. This makes Vienna much cheaper to live in than most big cities. “I live in a 100 square meter apartment from the early 1900s in a nice area, about 20 minutes’ walk from the city center. My rent is only €800 (£625) a month,” she said. In London, a similar apartment would cost more than £2,000, and it would be even more expensive in New York, which is number 44 on the list.

Cities in the United States did not do very well in the study, often because of problems with safety and crime. The top US city is San Francisco, at number 28. Boston comes in at number 34. Canadian cities do much better than American cities. Vancouver is the highest ranked Canadian city.

“You don’t know how safe Vienna is until you go to other countries,” Hartlauer said. “We also have great public transport. The subway works all night on weekends, and it costs just €1 for one trip.”

Clay Cockrell is sitting in his office at Columbus Circle, across the street from the Trump International Hotel and Tower at 1 Central Park West. In front of this building is Central Park, where Cockrell often does his walk-and-talk therapy sessions. 

Cockrell used to work on Wall Street but is now a therapist. He spends much of his day walking in Central Park or Battery Park near Wall Street. He talks to some of New York’s richest people and helps them with their problems.

“It just happened naturally,” he said about his work with rich clients. “Many very rich people—those in the top 1% of wealth—think their problems do not count. But they do have real problems. Many therapists do not take these problems seriously enough.”

So, what problems do the richest people in America have? “Some feel guilty for being rich. Some feel they have to hide their wealth. Also, being very rich can be lonely,” he explained.

It seems F. Scott Fitzgerald was right when he said the rich “are different from you and me.”

Therapists say things have become harder for the very rich since the financial crisis and with more people talking about income inequality. Movements like Occupy Wall Street and the Fight for $15 have made people focus more on rich people.

Jamie Traeger-Muney, a psychologist for wealthy people, said, “The Occupy Wall Street movement was good and brought attention to income inequality, but it made it seem like all rich people are bad. The media also makes the rich feel like they should hide or feel ashamed. Sometimes I am surprised by the things people say. You would never talk about other groups of people the way you talk about the rich.”

Swedish prisons are known around the world for being modern and kind to prisoners. But some people wonder if prisons in Sweden are too easy. In November 2013, Nils Oberg, who is the leader of Sweden’s prison and probation service, said that four prisons in Sweden would close. This is because there were fewer prisoners than before. The number of prisoners has dropped a lot, even though crime rates have not gone down. From 2011 to 2012, the number of people in prison in Sweden fell by 6%, and now there are just over 4,500 prisoners. The same kind of decrease was expected in 2013 and 2014. Oberg said he was surprised, but he hoped it was because Swedish prisons do a good job of helping prisoners not to commit crimes again.

Kenneth Gustafsson, the governor of Kumla Prison, which is the most secure prison in Sweden, said that prisons are different now from when he started working in 1978. Back then, prisoners were treated very well, maybe even too well. But after some famous escapes in 2004, they had to make the prisons safer. However, Sweden still tries to treat prisoners fairly and humanely, even if they have committed serious crimes. Prison sentences in Sweden are usually not longer than ten years.

Sweden was the first country in Europe to use electronic tags on some criminals. They also try not to put people in prison unless they really have to. Instead, they use community punishments, which have been shown to stop people from committing more crimes better than short prison sentences. In Sweden, about 30 to 40% of former prisoners commit another crime within three years. In the UK, this number is about twice as high.

One reason for the lower rate of repeat crime and fewer people in prison in Sweden is that people are only responsible for their crimes from age 15. In the UK, children as young as ten can be punished. Young people in the UK are more likely to commit more crimes and end up in adult prison. In Sweden, young people under 21 cannot get a life sentence, and the country tries hard to keep young offenders out of prison.

Many of us know we do not get enough sleep, but what if we could fix this easily by getting up later? Dr. Paul Kelley from Oxford University spoke at the British Science Festival. He said schools should start at different times to match students’ natural body clocks. This would help students think better, get better test scores, and be healthier. Lack of sleep is linked to problems like diabetes, depression, obesity, and a weak immune system.

Dr. Kelley wrote a paper in 2014. He said that when children are about ten years old, their bodies wake up at about 6:30am. At 16, their bodies wake up at 8am. At 18, teenagers naturally wake up around 9am. So, the usual school starting time is okay for 10-year-olds, but not for 16- to 18-year-olds. For older teenagers, it makes more sense to start school at 11am or later. 

Dr. Kelley also said that waking up at 7am for older teenagers feels the same as waking up at 4:30am for a teacher in their 50s. He explained that telling teenagers to sleep earlier does not work, because the body’s clock is controlled by a special kind of light. The eye has special cells that send signals to a part of the brain called the suprachiasmatic nuclei in the hypothalamus. This part of the brain controls our daily rhythms, or circadian rhythms. It is like asking someone to control their heartbeat—you cannot control it just by wanting to.
